2603.14672.title: Seamless Deception: Larger Language Models Are Better Knowledge Concealers
2603.14672.paper_summary: Language Models (LMs) may acquire harmful knowledge, and yet feign ignorance of these topics when under audit. Inspired by the recent discovery of deception-related behaviour patterns in LMs, we aim to train classifiers that detect when a LM is actively concealing knowledge. Initial findings on smaller models show that classifiers can detect concealment more reliably than human evaluators, with gradient-based concealment proving easier to identify than prompt-based methods. However, contrary to prior work, we find that the classifiers do not reliably generalize to unseen model architectures and topics of hidden knowledge. Most concerningly, the identifiable traces associated with concealment become fainter as the models increase in scale, with the classifiers achieving no better than random performance on any model exceeding 70 billion parameters. Our results expose a key limitation in black-box-only auditing of LMs and highlight the need to develop robust methods to detect models that are actively hiding the knowledge they contain.
2603.14672.paper_summary_zh: 語言模型（LMs）可能會獲得有害的知識，但在審核時卻假裝對這些主題無知。受到最近發現的與欺騙相關的行為模式的啟發，我們旨在訓練分類器，以檢測何時 LM 正在主動隱藏知識。對較小模型的初步研究顯示，分類器能比人類評估者更可靠地檢測隱藏行為，基於梯度的隱藏比基於提示的方法更容易識別。然而，與之前的研究相反，我們發現分類器無法可靠地泛化到未見過的模型架構和隱藏知識的主題。最令人擔憂的是，與隱藏行為相關的可識別痕跡在模型規模增大時變得越來越微弱，對於任何超過 700 億參數的模型，分類器的表現不過是隨機水平。我們的結果揭示了僅依賴黑箱審核 LM 的一個關鍵限制，並強調了開發可靠方法以檢測主動隱藏其所包含知識的模型的必要性。
2603.14672.author: Dhananjay Ashok et.al.
2603.14672.authors: Dhananjay Ashok, Ruth-Ann Armstrong, Jonathan May
